2604.21469.title: Cross-Domain Data Selection and Augmentation for Automatic Compliance Detection
2604.21469.paper_summary: Automating the detection of regulatory compliance remains a challenging task due to the complexity and variability of legal texts. Models trained on one regulation often fail to generalise to others. This limitation underscores the need for principled methods to improve cross-domain transfer. We study data selection as a strategy to mitigate negative transfer in compliance detection framed as a natural language inference (NLI) task. Specifically, we evaluate four approaches for selecting augmentation data from a larger source domain: random sampling, Moore-Lewis's cross-entropy difference, importance weighting, and embedding-based retrieval. We systematically vary the proportion of selected data to analyse its effect on cross-domain adaptation. Our findings demonstrate that targeted data selection substantially reduces negative transfer, offering a practical path toward scalable and reliable compliance automation across heterogeneous regulations.
2604.21469.paper_summary_zh: 自動化監管合規的檢測仍然是一項具有挑戰性的任務，因為法律文本的複雜性和變異性。<lb/>在一項法規上訓練的模型往往無法推廣到其他法規。<lb/>這一限制凸顯了改進跨領域轉移的原則性方法的必要性。<lb/>我們研究數據選擇作為減輕合規檢測中負面轉移的一種策略，將其框架設置為自然語言推理（NLI）任務。<lb/>具體而言，我們評估了四種從更大源域中選擇增強數據的方法：隨機抽樣、Moore-Lewis的交叉熵差異、重要性加權和基於嵌入的檢索。<lb/>我們系統性地改變所選數據的比例，以分析其對跨領域適應的影響。<lb/>我們的研究結果表明，針對性的數據選擇顯著減少了負面轉移，為在異質法規之間實現可擴展和可靠的合規自動化提供了一條實用的途徑。
2604.21469.author: Fariz Ikhwantri et.al.
